feed.title: Steffen Klug
feed.subtitle: Personal site of Steffen Klug — Cloud Architect. Blog posts, projects, architectures & diagrams, books & trainings, and a scrollable CV.

entry.title: Welcome — and what this site is for

link.title: Welcome — and what this site is for

entry.content:
This is a small home for things I write, build, and teach as a cloud architect.

You’ll find:

  • Blog posts — short, opinionated notes from real projects.
  • Projects — selected work I’m proud of (or learned the most from).
  • Architectures & Diagrams — reusable references for cloud and platform design.
  • Books & Trainings — what I read and recommend.
  • CV — a scrollable timeline of my career.

The goal is simple: write things down so they’re easier to share — and easier to revisit later.
entry.summary: This is a small home for things I write, build, and teach as a cloud architect.

entry.title: Azure landing zones, without the dogma

link.title: Azure landing zones, without the dogma

entry.content:
Landing zones are great — until they become a checklist no one understands. This is a short take on which guardrails are worth enforcing, which ones to treat as defaults, and which ones to leave entirely to the teams using the platform.

“Make the right thing easy and the wrong thing visible.”

More to come.
entry.summary: Landing zones are great — until they become a checklist no one understands. This is a short take on which guardrails are worth enforcing, which ones to treat as defaults, and which ones to leave entirely to the teams using the platform.
